key_insight: Orthogonalized enriched finite elements combine finite-element completeness with atom-centered efficiency to make large-scale pseudopotential DFT faster and more memory-efficient.
engineering_relevance: This framework can reduce the computational and memory burden of Kohn-Sham DFT for large non-periodic systems, enabling more practical high-fidelity simulations on available HPC resources.
method: Augment classical finite elements with compact atom-centered enrichment functions.
Orthogonalize the enrichment functions against the finite-element basis to improve conditioning and overlap-matrix inversion.
Use a residual-based Chebyshev subspace iteration that tolerates approximate inverse-overlap evaluations.
evidence_signal: Benchmarks up to 39,083 electrons show a 5–7× reduction in degrees of freedom, 5–9× lower computational cost, and 4–5× less memory than the classical finite-element basis.
watch_item: Performance depends on accurate orthogonalization and stable approximate overlap inversion, so implementation details may affect conditioning and scalability in new systems.
paper_title: Large-scale pseudopotential density functional theory calculations using orthogonalized enriched finite element basis
